result:
Done. The notes REST API is built, tested end-to-end, and committed.

## What I built

A dependency-free JSON REST API in Node.js (ESM):

- **`src/db.js`** — persistence layer using the built-in `node:sqlite` (`DatabaseSync`), writing to a real on-disk SQLite file at `data/notes.db` (overridable via `DB_PATH`). Creates the table on startup and exposes prepared-statement CRUD helpers.
- **`src/server.js`** — HTTP server using built-in `node:http`. Manual routing for all five endpoints, JSON body parsing with size limit, and strict validation.
- **`package.json`** — added `"start": "node src/server.js"` and a Node engines floor.
- **`.gitignore`** — ignores the `data/` database directory.
- **`README.md`** — run instructions + endpoint table.

## Behavior verified (live tests)

| Case | Result |
|------|--------|
| POST valid | `201` with server `id` (UUID) + `createdAt` (ISO 8601) |
| POST empty title / missing body / malformed JSON | `400` |
| GET /notes | `200` array |
| GET /notes/:id present / missing | `200` / `404` |
| PUT partial update / empty field / missing id | `200` / `400` / `404` |
| DELETE present / missing | `204` / `404` |
| **Restart persistence** | note created before restart was returned after restarting the server ✅ |

No external dependencies (no `npm install` needed). Listens on `PORT` (default 3000).

Committed on branch `feat/notes-crud-api` (I branched off `main` rather than committing directly to the default branch). I scoped the commit to just the API files, leaving the pre-existing unrelated `.agents/` deletions in the working tree untouched.
